name: Homemade Hamburger Buns ABM
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon malt powder or syrup, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine water, yeast, and a pinch of sugar in the bread machine pan. Let stand for a couple of minutes.
2. Add remaining ingredients in order: oil, additional sugar, salt, malt powder (optional), and milk.
3. Set machine to dough mode and start the cycle.
4. When the cycle is complete, remove the dough from the machine.
5. Gently deflate the dough and divide it into 8-10 portions. Cover with a clean tea towel and let rest for five minutes.
6. Shape each portion into a ball and place them evenly on a parchment paper-lined baking sheet.
7. Place the entire sheet in a large plastic bag (like a garbage bag) and allow to rise until quite puffy, about 20-30 minutes.
8. Remove from the plastic bag and gently flatten each roll with the palm of your hand.
9. Brush with milk if desired and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
10. Preheat oven to 375°F. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until nicely browned.
11. Freeze leftovers for future use.
This recipe yields approximately 8-10 rolls or buns. Malt powder can be found in health food stores or substituted with a teaspoon of barley malt syrup from home beer brewing stores or health food stores. King Arthur Flour catalog sells dried and syrup forms.
